title: Fuel price hike: Private schools in Bengaluru plan to increase transport fee
content: Parents of school-going children, already burdened by rising fuel prices, said a hike in the school transport fee would strain their budgets further

title: Morning flights from Bengaluru to Kalaburagi by Star Air starting June 10
content: The timing was changed following representations made by the Kalyana Karnataka Chamber of Commerce and Industry (KKCCI)

title: CM Vijay’s signature suit: Has Tamil Nadu’s new Chief Minister sparked a blazer trend?
content: From T Nagar clothing stores to custom tailoring studios, we explore whether Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay’s black-and-white suit has inspired a new fashion trend in Tamil Nadu, and how much it costs to recreate his look

title: Karnataka politics LIVE: Karnataka Governor in Indore as CM Siddaramaiah meets Cabinet colleagues today
content: It is being speculated that Chief Minister Siddaramaiah would announce his decision post the breakfast meeting

title: Jamiat chief says do not sacrifice prohibited animals, avoid sharing slaughter photos on social media
content: Former Vice-President Hamid Ansari also appealed to Muslims not to slaughter cows during Id-ul-Fitr and urged the Centre to heed the Jamiat chief's suggestion to declare the cow as the national animal

title: Id prayers disallowed at Srinagar's Jama Masjid for eighth year in a row
content: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq said it is very unfortunate that children in Kashmir are growing up without witnessing the spiritually uplifting Id prayers at the Eidgah

title: Bihar cabinet approves ‘cashless treatment’ for MLAs, government officials, employees and pensioners
content: The cabinet also approved the framing of “Bihar Ryot Land Purchase Policy 2026”, as per which land can be acquired based on mutual talks/coordination/consent from the ryots for various projects of both the central and state governments.

title: Annapurna Yojana: How to enrol in West Bengal’s new cash aid scheme for women
content: Under the Annapura Yojana scheme, women will receive ₹3,000 per month. The existing Lakshmir Bhandar scheme will continue until the new programme is fully implemented

title: WhatsApp, training and tenecteplase injection bring heart attack care to rural Punjab
content: By combining WhatsApp-based coordination, training for frontline healthcare workers, and the clot-busting injection tenecteplase, doctors are improving access to timely heart attack care in rural Punjab

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Madhya Pradesh HC quashes anticipatory bail of retired judge Giribala Singh
content: Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, representing the Madhya Pradesh Government, argued that “the manner in which anticipatory bail was obtained creates doubt that the trial Court did not consider the aspect relevant for granting anticipatory bail”

title: Ban cow slaughter in Tamil Nadu with immediate effect, orders Madras High Court
content: Judges say, goats and sheep too should not be allowed to be slaughtered in any place other than a licensed slaughter house

title: Karnataka will go to mid-term polls, says BJP State president B.Y. Vijayendra
content: Mr. Vijayendra said the Congress high command’s marathon meeting in Delhi, which culminated in a directive to Siddaramaiah to step down, was an admission of the government’s failure.

title: ‘I will speak tomorrow’: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah says, amid talk of power transition
content: Party sources indicated that the Congress Legislative Party meeting, where the likely successor of Mr. Siddaramaiah could be named or elected, is likely to held on May 28.

title: Isolated Bengaluru passenger tests negative for Ebola
content: India currently has no confirmed cases of the Ebola virus disease till date, the Union Health Ministry says, all necessary public health protocols are being followed in accordance with WHO guidelines

title: India sends emergency medical supplies for Ebola outbreak response in Congo
content: India has, in recent years, expanded its health and development partnership with African countries through the supply of medicines, vaccines and medical assistance, particularly during public health emergencies such as the COVID-19 pandemic

title: Supreme Court refuses urgent hearing of PIL seeking complete ban on cow slaughter
content: The plea seeks directions to enforce anti-slaughter laws to protect the cow and its progeny from slaughter

title: Boycott of cattle market in Hassan to extend to other parts of Karnataka, pressure to be maintained on govt. to repeal law
content: The federation is now in contact with community leaders across other districts to expand the campaign Statewide

title: Supreme Court to examine if CBSE’s three-language rule is ‘unreasonable’ on kids, resources
content: Three-judge Bench headed by the Chief Justice of India Surya Kant says the court is more concerned with the logistical and factual challenges of implementing the policy in the face of a dearth of teachers and books

title: NCW calls for legislation to codify Muslim personal law related to marriage
content: The report recommended prohibition of child marriage and regulation of divorce practices, ensuring fairness, due process, and equal access to remedies for women

title: Respite from heat expected over weekend
content: Delhiites are expected to get some respite from the heat with more rains expected over Friday, (May 29, 2026), Saturday May 30, and May 31,

title: No specific port project identified, says Fiji’s Foreign Minister, day after Quad Foreign Ministers pledge assistance
content: In a written response to questions from The Hindu, Mr. Ditoka said Fiji’s approach to developing its infrastructure is driven by plans to create better facilities that will support its commercial ambitions

title: Rate at which West Bengal tribunals restoring names of deleted electors proves genuine voters not allowed to vote: Prasenjit Bose
content: Prasenjit Bose serves as the chairperson, SIR Committee of West Bengal Pradesh Congress Committee emphasised that with over 60 % electors restored to voter list by the tribunals it is clear that genuine electors  were deprived of their right to vote in the 2026 West Bengal assembly election

title: Probe incidents of accused being paraded in underwear: APDR writes to West Bengal Human Rights Commission
content: Association of Protection of Democratic Rights (APDR), a well known civil rights organisation in a letter to WBHRC has highlighted two instances at Golabari police station on May 24 and at Sankrail police station on May 25, where two accused were paraded in their underwear.

title: Piped drinking water in remaining Mahadalit Tolas in the next three months: Bihar Govt
content: A total of 2.66 lakh families were getting piped drinking water in 2016, which has increased to 1.87 crore families in 2026. It has now set the target of providing piped drinking water to 2.02 crore families in the rural areas.

title: Priority will be given to river linking project: Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde
content: He called the “the river linking project” a boon for Maharashtra, especially Marathwada and other drought-affected areas

title: TDP leverages technology in organising hybrid Mahanadu
content: Party conceptualises the hybrid architecture under the guidance of its national working president Nara Lokesh to make participation more inclusive, scalable and cost-efficient, while enabling real-time engagement from across the State

title: Anti-drug surveillance stepped up ahead of school reopening in Ernakulam
content: Preliminary steps have already been initiated in coordination with school protection groups, parents, and other stakeholders, says District Police Chief (Kochi City) Kaliraj Mahesh Kumar

title: Leadership change in Karnataka: All eyes on CM Siddaramaiah’s breakfast meeting with Cabinet colleagues on May 28
content: A day after being asked by the Congress high command to move to the Rajya Sabha, thus paving the way for a leadership change, speculation over his next move is likely to come to rest as he is expected to spell out during the breakfast meeting.

title: Vellore Corporation demolishes encroachments at the new bus terminus
content: Officials of Vellore Corporation, which executed the demolition drive, said that the civic body had received regular complaints from commuters that they were unable to board buses safely due to illegal stalls in the waiting area

title: BIS organises awareness session for footwear manufacturers
content: The programme aimed to create awareness among MSMEs and stakeholders from the footwear and leather sector regarding Indian Standards and Quality Control Orders

title: Will file curative petition against youngster’s acquittal from 2017 Mugalivakkam child sexual assault-cum-murder case, T.N. govt. tells Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan close a writ petition after recording the submission

title: Rest still a favour, not a right, for retail-staff in Chennai
content: Five years after Tamil Nadu legislated on the ‘right to sit’ provision within the Tamil Nadu Shops and Establishments Act, 1947, the law remains a luxury for many. From high-end coffee shops to bustling textile showrooms, Chennai’s retail workers continue to trade their long-term health for long shifts on their feet

title: Police report flags suspected gold thefts at Sree Padmanabhaswamy Temple
content: The primary findings detail that nearly 78 grams of gold bars and coins, donated to the shrine as offerings by devotees, have gone missing recently

title: Congress forms committee to look into allegations of anti-party activities
content: Speaking to reporters, former MP K. Jayakumar, said that the committee would investigate and look into the allegations and present a report in 60 days

title: Supreme Court upholds SIR exercise as an advancement towards free and fair elections
content: Court says EC can verify citizenship status for inclusion or exclusion from the electoral roll; it directs poll body to refer the names of Bihar electors purged from the rolls to the Centre for adjudication under the Citizenship Act

title: Trinamool MLA arrested, several leaders quit party post
content: The Trinamool leadership is  facing another challenge that despite being the principle Opposition party in the State with 80 MLAs, no room has been allocated Leader of Opposition Sovandeb Chattopadhyay in the premises of State Assembly

title: Union Minister stresses need to advance space technology in India
content: Speaking at the launch of Mission STRATO-XI, a near-space technology demonstration conducted at IGMC Stadium in Vijayawada, Ram Mohan Naidu describes the event as a landmark achievement

title: Karnataka CM power play: DKS sought Priyanka’s help after no green signal from Rahul
content: D.K. Shivakumar's bid for the Chief Minister post gained traction after Priyanka Gandhi Vadra intervened, persuading Rahul and Sonia Gandhi. Siddaramaiah reportedly conveyed his decision to resign, though he is hesitant about a national role. A press conference is expected before his resignation, with Shivakumar attending a breakfast meeting.

title: 2 suspected Ebola cases trigger airport surveillance, quarantine measures across nation
content: Following reports of suspected Ebola infections in Bengaluru and Gujarat, Indian health officials have intensified surveillance at airports and introduced stringent quarantine measures. A Ugandan woman has fortunately tested negative in Bengaluru, while a 37-year-old man in Gujarat is currently being watched closely after traveling from Africa and Bangladesh.

title: 'Haters will say you're not 15': Sooryavanshi turns heads with record knock
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s stunning IPL 2026 season continued with a blistering 97 off 29 balls against SRH, including 12 sixes. The 15-year-old broke Chris Gayle’s record for most sixes in a T20 tournament. Cricket legends Sachin Tendulkar, Andre Russell, Yuvraj Singh and Mohammed Kaif praised the RR star, who now has 680 runs and multiple IPL records this season.

title: Three months of Hormuz chaos: Oil prices jump 3% amid US-Iran standoff
content: Oil prices saw a dip as hopes for a US-Iran deal to ease Middle East tensions and reopen the Strait of Hormuz emerged. Despite earlier drops, crude prices are recovering slightly. President Trump emphasized his focus on preventing Iran's nuclear ambitions and ensuring the Strait's accessibility, warning against interference.

title: American billionaire Mark Cuban sells 'most of' his Bitcoin
content: Mark Cuban, a former crypto evangelist, has sold most of his Bitcoin, stating it failed as a hedge against global turmoil. He found gold performed better during geopolitical events, questioning Bitcoin's role. Cuban also shared advice on AI use for students, emphasizing critical thinking over reliance.

title: Eid-ul-Adha: Top 100 wishes and quotes for Bakrid
content: Navigating the flurry of Eid-ul-Adha greetings can be overwhelming. This guide offers 115 ready-to-use messages, from heartfelt family wishes and foodie friend prompts to professional greetings and quick replies.

title: Cloudflare lays off 1,100 employees; CEO says company isn’t struggling
content: Cloudflare is laying off over 1,100 employees, about 20% of its workforce, to restructure around AI adoption. CEO Matthew Prince explained the cuts target 'measurers' – roles like middle management and operations – as AI enhances efficiency. He believes AI will boost productivity for 'builders' and 'sellers', allowing greater investment in growth areas.

title: Sooryavanshi touches Gavaskar’s feet, then smashes 97 vs SRH - Watch
content: Teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi impressed on and off the field during IPL 2026. He touched Sunil Gavaskar's feet before an Eliminator match and later smashed a blistering 97 off 29 balls, powering Rajasthan Royals to a 47-run victory.

title: Exclusive: IPL chairman opens up on honey-trap concerns, calls it 'very clean tournament'
content: IPL governing council chairman Arun Dhumal distanced himself from speculation surrounding BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia's 'honey-trap' remark, saying no such incident had surfaced during IPL 2026 and described the tournament as a 'very clean' season.

title: Hormuz standoff: Trump's 'will blow them up' warning to Oman amid fragile Iran-US talks
content: Speaking to reporters during a cabinet meeting at the White House on Wednesday local time, Trump rejected the idea of any temporary arrangement that would allow Iran and Oman to jointly manage the strait. "No, the strait's going to be open to everybody," Trump said when asked whether he would support such a deal. Calling it an international passage, the US President stressed that no country would be allowed to dominate it.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: Mamata Banerjee lambasts rebels with 'Girgiti' poem after Bengal poll rout
content: Facing internal dissent after a poll setback, Mamata Banerjee penned a poem, 'Girgiti (Chameleon),' targeting rebel leaders. The verses accuse them of changing character for personal gain and warn of consequences. This comes as several leaders resign from posts, citing 'VVIP culture' and leadership failures amid controversies, intensifying the party's crisis.

title: Several feared dead as boat carrying 14 capsizes in Ganga near Patna; 2 bodies found
content: A boat carrying approximately 14 people capsized in the Ganga river near Barh, Patna, due to strong winds. Two bodies have been recovered, and five individuals are still missing. Rescue operations are underway to find the remaining passengers.

title: After 17 state budgets and 2 CM terms, Siddaramaiah stares at toughest political decision
content: As Karnataka approaches a new political era, the esteemed Chief Minister Siddaramaiah stands on the brink of retirement, leaving behind a legacy that includes crafting 17 state budgets and championing the rights of marginalized groups. His two-decade journey has not only redefined welfare policies but also solidified the Congress party's influence in the state's politics.

title: KCET 2026 result date: When will KEA release UGCET scorecards?
content: Karnataka Common Entrance Test (KCET) 2026 results are expected soon on cetonline.karnataka.gov.in, likely after June 1st. Over 3.3 lakh candidates can check their scorecards online using application number and name. The exam, held April 22-24, had no negative marking. Students must upload marks for result preparation. A rank list and subsequent admission process will follow.

title: ‘Pre-trial detention can’t be indefinite’: Allahabad HC grants bail in pro-Pak post case
content: The Allahabad High Court granted bail to Tahir Mewati, accused of posting anti-India and pro-Pakistan content online. The court emphasized that pre-trial detention cannot be indefinite, even for grave charges, upholding constitutional rights. Mewati, in jail since May 2025, claims false implication and a fake social media account.

title: Twisha Sharma death: CBI may take mother-in-law in custody after HC scraps bail
content: The Madhya Pradesh High Court has revoked the anticipatory bail granted to Giribala Singh, mother-in-law of deceased Twisha Sharma. The court found that the trial court failed to consider crucial evidence, including witness statements and postmortem findings, when granting the bail. The CBI is expected to take Singh into custody for further investigation into allegations of cruelty and dowry.

title: Madness in Mullanpur: Sooryavanshi's 97 was not power-hitting alone, it was...
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi produced an innings that felt like a glimpse into cricket's future, smashing 97 off just 29 balls in the IPL Eliminator, leaving Sunrisers Hyderabad with no answers. Though he fell three runs short of a record-breaking century, the 15-year-old's fearless strokeplay, tactical maturity and breathtaking power ensured Mullanpur witnessed an innings that may live forever in IPL folklore.

title: Historic for India! Praggnanandhaa shocks Magnus Carlsen in Norway; 3rd win for Divya
content: India's R Praggnanandhaa scripted a sensational repeat of history at the Norway Chess tournament in Oslo, stunning local hero and World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen in a 62-move classical encounter. Meanwhile, debutante Divya Deshmukh continued her dream run in the women's section, defeating tournament leader Bibisara Assaubayeva to secure her third consecutive Armageddon victory.

title: Taylor Swift suffers shocking AMA loss ahead of wedding to Travis Kelce
content: Taylor Swift experienced an unexpected setback after failing to secure any wins despite entering a major music event with the highest number of nominations. The outcome sparked widespread discussion among fans online, with some questioning the results while others argued the singer has already achieved more than enough success to cement her legacy. Meanwhile, supporters and critics continued debating the surprising development.

title: Cowboys rumored to eye blockbuster trade for Raiders' $100M Maxx Crosby
content: The Dallas Cowboys continue to be linked with Las Vegas Raiders star Maxx Crosby in trade speculation. Reports suggest the Raiders previously sought two first-round picks in exchange for the All-Pro pass rusher. While no deal appears imminent, Crosby remains a potential target for Dallas as it looks to strengthen its defense. Any trade would require significant draft capital and salary-cap maneuvering, making it a challenging but impactful move.

title: NFL Rumors: Chiefs linked to Giants’ Pass Rusher & Bears’ TE in ESPN Trades
content: The Kansas City Chiefs have been linked to trade rumors involving New York Giants pass rusher Kayvon Thibodeaux and Chicago Bears tight end Cole Kmet. ESPN proposed deals involving future draft picks, but both were viewed as unlikely. Analysts believe the Giants and Bears have little reason to move key contributors. While Thibodeaux could strengthen Kansas City's pass rush and Kmet could help at tight end, salary cap concerns and long-term roster planning make both potential trades difficult.

title: Stock market holiday on Bakri Id: Are BSE, NSE closed for trading today?
content: Dalal Street participants will experience a shortened trading week as both NSE and BSE will be closed on Thursday, May 28, for Bakri Id. This holiday follows the Maharashtra Day closure and is one of 16 scheduled for 2026. Commodity markets will have a partial closure, with MCX resuming evening trade while NCDEX remains shut.

title: 'New six machine': Gayle makes Sooryavanshi admission
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi starred in Rajasthan Royals’ IPL 2026 Eliminator win over Sunrisers Hyderabad with a blazing 97 off 29 balls, including 12 sixes. The 15-year-old broke Chris Gayle’s record for most sixes in an IPL season. Gayle called him the “new six machine,” while Suryakumar Yadav praised his fearless batting. RR now face Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2.

title: 'What I'm doing is dangerous business': Donald Trump after White House shooting
content: President Trump acknowledged the dangers of public office, stating he doesn't dwell on  threats to effectively lead. This comes after recent security scares, including a shooting near the White House Correspondents' Dinner and an incident at his Mar-a-Lago resort.  He emphasized that consequential leaders face significant risks in a dangerous business.

title: Bengaluru techie who quit corporate job for farming killed by lightning in paddy field
content: A Kodagu resident, IT employee Hosoklu Roshan Balakrishna, was tragically killed by lightning while inspecting paddy fields near Mysuru. In a separate incident, Mathari Harsha, 37, from Kodagu, died of a heart attack while returning by train from a tribal conference in Delhi.

title: Ketel Marte emerges as Yankees’ top trade target before MLB deadline
content: The Yankees are being strongly linked with Diamondbacks star Ketel Marte ahead of the 2026 MLB trade deadline. Marte’s $116.5 million contract, strong hitting numbers, and All-Star experience make him one of the biggest possible trade targets this season. However, his no-trade list and Arizona’s asking price could make any blockbuster deal difficult for New York to complete.

title: ‘Centuries will keep happening’: Sooryavanshi after missing fastest IPL ton
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi delivered a stunning 97 off 29 balls to guide Rajasthan Royals past Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL 2026 Eliminator. His explosive innings featured 12 sixes, nearly breaking a record. Sooryavanshi remains focused on the team's trophy pursuit, emphasizing simple preparation and playing the game ahead.

title: Sooryavanshi scripts history, becomes first batter to...
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi etched his name in IPL history with a blistering 97 off 29 balls in the 2026 Eliminator. The Rajasthan Royals opener shattered multiple records, including Chris Gayle's long-standing mark for most sixes in a season with 65. Sooryavanshi also set new benchmarks for powerplay runs and sixes in an innings, showcasing extraordinary talent.

title: Madras high court bans cow, calf slaughter in Tamil Nadu
content: Madras high court has issued a blanket ban on the slaughter of cows and calves in Tamil Nadu, not just for Bakrid but on any day. The court directed state authorities to ensure no such slaughter occurs, citing constitutional mandates and existing government orders aimed at protecting cattle and improving the rural economy.

title: ‘Emily in Paris’ star Pierre Deny passes away
content: The iconic Pierre Deny, renowned for his portrayal of Louis de Léon in 'Emily in Paris,' has tragically passed away at the age of 69 following a courageous battle with ALS. With an impressive legacy of over 100 roles in film and television, Deny was celebrated not only for his incredible talent but also for his warmth and kindness.

title: ED raids ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi home in case tied to daughter, sparks outrage
content: CPM workers attack ED team’s vehicles with bricks and sticks as officials leave Pinarayi residence after eight-and-a-half-hour search; driver hospitalised with eye injury, four arrested

title: Vijay receives guard of honour at Tamil Nadu house
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay met Prime Minister Narendra Modi, discussing key state issues including the Vande Mataram controversy and the Mekedatu project. This marked their first public meeting in over a decade.  Vijay also received a guard of honour at Tamil Nadu House. Union Minister Suresh Gopi urged patience for the new CM.

title: Iran Attacks US Base In Retaliation For Strikes Near Bandar Abbas: Report
content: The Iranian Revolutionary Guards targeted an American base on Thursday in retaliation for US strikes on the country's south, Iran's...

title: "No One Will Control Hormuz": Trump Warns As US, Iran "Negotiating On Fumes"
content: Round after round of talks have ended without conclusion, leading a frustrated Trump to say the Iranian side is "negotiating on fumes".

title: Vijay Won't Meet Gandhis Today, New Date Likely Next Week: Sources
content: The meeting was to take place at 10 am, but was postponed late Wednesday night, though the reasons behind the same remain unclear.

title: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: Siddaramaiah-DK Shivakumar Breakfast Meet To Begin Shortly
content: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: A change in leadership in the three-year-old Karnataka government appears imminent, with Siddaramaiah having sought an appointment with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot, reportedly to tender his resignation.

title: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Live Updates: Vijay's Meeting With Rahul, Sonia Gandhi Postponed
content: Vijay Delhi Visit Live Updates: Vijay assumed office as Chief Minister on May 10 after his party emerged as the single largest party in the Assembly elections. TVK had won 108 seats in the 234-member Assembly

title: 4 Cubs Die In Gujarat's Gir Forest Of Suspected Infection, 17 Lions Isolated
content: Four lion cubs die in Gir forest due to suspected infection while 17 lions are isolated and monitored.

title: Camel From Rajasthan Sparks Buzz In Assam Village On Eid
content: The camel, transported from Rajasthan via Kharupetia, arrived in Sissiborgaon, where it quickly became the centre of attraction.

title: "I Don't Care About Midterms": Trump Dismisses Iran War Fallout
content: "They thought they were going to outwait me," Trump said at a White House cabinet meeting, referring to Iran's leadership. "You know, 'We'll outwait him. He's got the midterms.' I don't care about the midterms."

title: Indian Living Illegally In US Arrested For Assaulting Man With Baseball Bat
content: An Indian national assaulted a man with a baseball bat and was released by sanctuary politicians without ICE alert.

title: Running Hookah Bar No Fundamental Right, State Can Restrict: Court
content: Allahabad High Court says hookah bars are not a fundamental right and can be restricted to protect public health

title: Missed Investing In Your 20s? Mutual Funds Can Help You Catch Up, Here's How
content: Starting late is not ideal. But it is not the end of the road either. In fact, people in their 30s and 40s often have an advantage -- higher income

title: Iran War LIVE Updates: US Strikes Iranian Military Site After 'Negotiating On Fumes' Warning
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: US President Donald Trump had on Wednesday dismissed an Iranian state media report that Iran and Oman would jointly manage shipping through the Strait of Hormuz

title: Kolkata's 70-Foot Messi Statue To Be Taken Down 5 Months After Installation
content: The 70-foot statue of legendary Argentine footballer Lionel Messi that installed in Kolkata is set to be taken down due to safety concerns.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Delhi May Get Relief As Weather Body Predicts Rain, Storms Today
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Light to very light rain is expected during the afternoon and evening, accompanied by strong winds and squalls reaching speeds of 50-60 kmph.

title: US To Set Up Ebola Quarantine Facility In Kenya For Americans
content: Donald Trump's administration is planning to send Americans who are exposed to Ebola while abroad to a new facility in Kenya instead of flying them to the United States, an administration official said Wednesday.

title: US-Iran War Highlights: US Army Carries Out New Strikes In Iran Against Military Site
content: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.

title: In Twisha Sharma Death Case, Court Rejects Giribala Singh's Bail
content: Twisha Sharma Death Case: Madhya Pradesh High Court on Wednesday quashed the anticipatory bail of former judge Giribala Singh in the Twisha Sharma death case.

content: US-Iran War Latest News: US Carries Out New Strikes In Iran, Intercepts Iranian Drones, Strait Of Hormuz

title: Assam clears UCC bill, Himanta says rooted in RSS, BJP ideology
content: Prabin Kalita is a journalist at The Times of India and is currently the Chief of Bureau (northeast). He has been reporting in mainstream Indian national media since 2001. He has been a field journalist reporting gamut of issues from India’s northeastern region and major developments in neighbouring countries like Myanmar, China, Bhutan and Bangladesh concerning India and northeastern region. He has been covering insurgency—internal and cross-border, politics, natural calamities, environment etc. He is a post-graduate in Geological Sciences from Gauhati University. Read More

title: Practise what you preach by carpooling, PM Modi advises ministers
content: toi learnt that his remarks came during a meeting of the union cabinet . recently, PM Modi had asked for his convoy to be trimmed and suggested ministers to reduce their fleet of vehicles . he also asked minists to switch to public transport to reduce consumption of petrol and diesel .

title: Astronomers identify ‘Loktak protocluster’ named after Loktak lake in Manipur
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
